steps.instruction: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two remote (non-adjacent) interior angles.
steps.work: Exterior angle = remote interior angle 1 + remote interior angle 2
steps.explanation: This follows from the triangle angle sum being 180°.

steps.instruction: A triangle has interior angles 50° and 70°. Find the exterior angle adjacent to the third angle.
steps.explanation: The exterior angle is the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles.

steps.instruction: The third interior angle = 180° − 50° − 70° = 60°. The exterior angle should be supplementary to this.
steps.explanation: An exterior angle and its adjacent interior angle are supplementary (sum to 180°).

title: Using Exterior Angle Theorem with Algebra

steps.instruction: Exterior angle = (4x)°. Remote interior angles = (x + 10)° and (2x − 5)°.
steps.explanation: Apply the exterior angle theorem: exterior = sum of remotes.
The exterior angle theorem is satisfied.
